verses.text: Humanity multiplied across the earth. The sons of God here are debated: some take it as fallen angelic beings, others as the godly line of Seth marrying outside it. Either way, the result is a deepening of the brokenness Genesis is tracking.

Humanity multiplied across the earth. The sons of God here are debated: some take it as fallen angelic beings, others as the godly line of Seth marrying outside it. Either way, the result is a deepening of the brokenness Genesis is tracking.
My Spirit shall not always strive with man — God sets a limit. His patience has an end. The 120 years here is most likely the countdown to the flood.
Nephilim — translated giants — appear in the land. These were men of renown but not of righteousness. The world has begun to celebrate strength and fame without God.

God sees: every intent of the human heart was only evil continually. And it grieved Him. The God who blessed creation now repents that He made humanity. Sin doesn't just break the world; it grieves God Himself.

God sees: every intent of the human heart was only evil continually. And it grieved Him. The God who blessed creation now repents that He made humanity. Sin doesn't just break the world; it grieves God Himself.

God sees: every intent of the human heart was only evil continually. And it grieved Him. The God who blessed creation now repents that He made humanity. Sin doesn't just break the world; it grieves God Himself.
But Noah found grace in the eyes of the LORD. The flood narrative pivots on this one sentence. Even in the worst generation, God preserves one family — and through them, hope.
Noah was a just man, perfect in his generations — meaning blameless among his contemporaries. He walked with God, the same phrase used of Enoch. He had three sons: Shem, Ham, Japheth.
Noah was a just man, perfect in his generations — meaning blameless among his contemporaries. He walked with God, the same phrase used of Enoch. He had three sons: Shem, Ham, Japheth.
The earth was corrupted before God, and filled with violence. The same word — corrupted — is repeated three times in this short passage. The repetition is intentional: this is total ruin.
The earth was corrupted before God, and filled with violence. The same word — corrupted — is repeated three times in this short passage. The repetition is intentional: this is total ruin.
God speaks directly to Noah: The end of all flesh has come before me. The flood is not chaos returning; it is judgment from the throne.
God gives precise blueprints for the ark — its wood, its dimensions, its rooms, its single door, its window. Noah is told exactly how to build the vessel that will hold the future of the world.
God gives precise blueprints for the ark — its wood, its dimensions, its rooms, its single door, its window. Noah is told exactly how to build the vessel that will hold the future of the world.
God gives precise blueprints for the ark — its wood, its dimensions, its rooms, its single door, its window. Noah is told exactly how to build the vessel that will hold the future of the world.
And, behold, I, even I, do bring a flood. God speaks His own intention twice in this sentence — I, even I. There is no doubt about who is acting.
But I will establish my covenant with thee. This is the first time the word covenant appears in the Bible. Even in judgment, God is making a binding promise to save.
Two of every living thing must come into the ark — male and female. The animals come to Noah; he does not have to hunt them. God is gathering His own creation to safety.
Two of every living thing must come into the ark — male and female. The animals come to Noah; he does not have to hunt them. God is gathering His own creation to safety.
Noah is also to gather food. And the chapter closes with a stunning line: Thus did Noah; according to all that God commanded him, so did he. Faith looks like obedience.
Noah is also to gather food. And the chapter closes with a stunning line: Thus did Noah; according to all that God commanded him, so did he. Faith looks like obedience.
